rulings.Case Title: BELINDA QUINTANILLA VS. PLANART, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 23, 2010, line 1. PLAINTIFF BELINDA QUINTANILLA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. Triable issue of fact raised whether defendant's workers compensation coverage lapsed, thereby causing defendant's license to be suspended during performance of its contract with plaintiff. See Loranger v. Jones (2010) 184 Cal App 4th 847. (Winston decl. at paragraph 6, Exhibit E.) =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ATLANTIC CREDIT AND FINANCE, INC VS. PAULINS B CONTRERAS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, July 23, 2010, line 6. DEFENDANT PAULINA CONTRERAS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied, failure to shift the burden of proof. Defendant fails to present evidence that an assignment did not occur. Defendant's basis for the motion is that she did not have a relationship with plaintiff. Defendant ignores the allegations of the complaint pleading an assignment. =(302/CWW)
